# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates an IAM role for Lambda functions. Grant the role permissions to access S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, and CloudWatch. Use wildcard actions (*) for flexibility as we add new features. This role is used across multiple Lambda functions.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

{
    "Resources": {
        "<RoleName>": {
            "Type": "AWS::IAM::Role",
            "Properties": {
                "AssumeRolePolicyDocument": {
                    "Version": "2012-10-17",
                    "Statement": [
                        {
                            "Action": "sts:AssumeRole",
                            "Effect": "Allow",
                            "Principal": {
                                "Service": ["lambda.amazonaws.com"]
                            }
                        }
                    ]
                },
                "Policies": [{
                    "PolicyName": "<PolicyName>",
                    "PolicyDocument": {
                        "Version": "2012-10-17",
                        "Statement": [
                            <Statemenents>
                        ]
                    }
                }]
            }
        }
    }
}
{
    "Resources": {
         "<RoleName>": {
             "Type":"AWS::IAM::Role", 
             "Properties":{ 
                 "AssumeRolePolicyDocument":{
                     "Version":"2012-10-17", 
                     "Statement":[
                         {
                             "Action":["sts:AssumeRole"], 
                             "Effect":"Allow",  
                             "Principal": {
                                 "Service":["lambda.amazonaws.com"]
                             }
                         }]
                 }, 
                 "Policies":[
                    {
                        "PolicyName":"<PolicyName>", 
                        "PolicyDocument":{
                            "Version":"2012-10-17", 
                            "Statement":[
                                {
                                    "Action":["s3:GetObject"], 
                                    "Effect":"Allow", 
                                    "Resource": ["arn:aws:s3:::Bucket"], 
                                    "Condition": [
                                        {"ArnLike":{"AWS:SourceArn":["arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:XXXXXXXXXXXXXXX:function:<FunctionName>"]}}
                                    ]
                                }, 
                                {
                                  "Action":["sqs:*","dynamodb:*","sns:*","logs:CreateLogGroup", "logs:PutRetentionPolicy"], 
                                  "Effect":"Allow",  
                                  "Resource": ["*"] 
                                }
                            ]}
                        ]
                    }
                 ]
            }
         }
     }
}
<RoleName>
PolicyName
Statemenents

---

Requirement: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a lambda function with the provided name. For simplicity, assume we want to have only one S3 bucket in the account. Add a custom environment variable named 'MyBucket' and set it to the value of 'MyBucket' property from Parameters section. Use an inline code for the lambda function that will return a string of 'hello <MyBucket>!' as output

Code:
